proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `braf_v600e` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `braf_v600e` equal to 0.
Cell lines with `egfr_amplification` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines without `egfr_amplification`.
Cell lines with `erbb2_amplification` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines without `erbb2_amplification`.
Cell lines with `kras_mutation` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines without `kras_mutation`.

analyses.code: Two-sample mean contrast of dependency_BRAF by braf_v600e.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_BRAF` averaged -0.8047 in `braf_v600e=1` lines (n=5574) versus -0.2495 in `braf_v600e=0` lines; the signed difference was -0.5552.

analyses.code: Two-sample mean contrast of dependency_EGFR by egfr_amplification.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_EGFR` averaged -0.6010 in `egfr_amplification=1` lines (n=3880) versus -0.2524 otherwise; the signed difference was -0.3486.

analyses.code: Two-sample mean contrast of dependency_ERBB2 by erbb2_amplification.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_ERBB2` averaged -0.6856 in `erbb2_amplification=1` lines (n=4890) versus -0.2535 otherwise; the signed difference was -0.4321.

analyses.code: Two-sample mean contrast of dependency_KRAS by kras_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KRAS` averaged -0.5594 in `kras_mutation=1` lines (n=11842) versus -0.2520 otherwise; the signed difference was -0.3075.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Skin-lineage cell lines have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than non-skin cell lines, consistent with enrichment for BRAF-pathway dependence.
Pancreatic-lineage cell lines have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than non-pancreatic cell lines, beyond the screen-wide average.
Brain-lineage cell lines have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than non-brain cell lines.

analyses.code: Lineage subgroup contrast for dependency_BRAF: lineage == 'skin' versus all other lineages.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_BRAF` was -0.4721 in skin lines (n=4935) versus -0.2938 in all other lines; effect -0.1783.

analyses.code: Lineage subgroup contrast for dependency_KRAS: lineage == 'pancreatic' versus all other lineages.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KRAS` was -0.4260 in pancreatic lines (n=5102) versus -0.3133 in all other lines; effect -0.1127.

analyses.code: Lineage subgroup contrast for dependency_EGFR: lineage == 'brain' versus all other lineages.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_EGFR` was -0.3307 in brain lines (n=3565) versus -0.2755 in all other lines; effect -0.0552.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines in the top quartile of `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (>=0.964) have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than slower-growing cell lines.
Cell lines in the top quartile of `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (>=0.964) have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than slower-growing cell lines.
Cell lines with `myc_amplification` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than cell lines without `myc_amplification`.
Cell lines in the top quartile of `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` (>=5.669) have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than lower-MYC cell lines.

analyses.code: Top-quartile subgroup contrast for growth_rate_doublings_per_day on dependency_POLR2A.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_POLR2A` was -0.4972 in high-growth lines (n=12577) versus -0.3928 in other lines; effect -0.1043.

analyses.code: Top-quartile subgroup contrast for growth_rate_doublings_per_day on dependency_TMED10.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_TMED10` was -0.2816 in high-growth lines (n=12577) versus -0.2447 in other lines; effect -0.0369.

analyses.code: Two-sample mean contrast of dependency_RPL11 by myc_amplification.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_RPL11` was -0.3614 in `myc_amplification=1` lines (n=8055) versus -0.2523 otherwise; effect -0.1091.

analyses.code: Top-quartile subgroup contrast for rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm on dependency_DHX9.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_DHX9` was -0.3551 in high-`rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` lines (n=12501) versus -0.3265 in other lines; effect -0.0286.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines in the top quartile of `aneuploidy_score` (>=0.478) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with lower `aneuploidy_score`.
Higher continuous `ploidy` is associated with more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores.

analyses.code: Top-quartile subgroup contrast for aneuploidy_score on dependency_KIF18A.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KIF18A` was -0.3078 in high-aneuploidy lines (n=12521) versus -0.3065 otherwise; effect -0.0013, not significant.

analyses.code: Pearson/linear slope of dependency_KIF18A on continuous ploidy.
analyses.result_summary: `ploidy` had a small negative slope with `dependency_KIF18A` (slope -0.0266, r=-0.0341); the large sample made it statistically significant but the magnitude was small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Colorectal-lineage cell lines have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
Cell lines with `apc_mutation` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines without `apc_mutation`.
Cell lines in the top quartile of `wnt_activity_score` (>=0.819) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than lower-WNT cell lines.
Cell lines in the top quartile of `rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm` (>=3.559) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than lower-ASCL2 cell lines.

analyses.code: Lineage subgroup contrast for dependency_KIF18A: lineage == 'colorectal' versus all other lineages.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KIF18A` was -0.7204 in colorectal lines (n=5816) versus -0.2524 in non-colorectal lines; effect -0.4680.

analyses.code: Two-sample mean contrast of dependency_KIF18A by apc_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KIF18A` was -0.5447 in `apc_mutation=1` lines (n=9285) versus -0.2526 otherwise; effect -0.2921.

analyses.code: Top-quartile subgroup contrast for wnt_activity_score on dependency_KIF18A.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KIF18A` was -0.4694 in high-WNT lines (n=12508) versus -0.2526 otherwise; effect -0.2168.

analyses.code: Top-quartile subgroup contrast for rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm on dependency_KIF18A.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KIF18A` was -0.3880 in high-ASCL2 lines (n=12510) versus -0.2797 otherwise; effect -0.1083.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strongest `dependency_KIF18A` subgroup is the joint subgroup `lineage=colorectal`, `apc_mutation=1`, and `wnt_activity_score>=0.819`, with more negative `dependency_KIF18A` than all other cell lines.
Within colorectal cell lines, either absence of `apc_mutation` or `wnt_activity_score<0.819` suppresses the strong negative `dependency_KIF18A` phenotype.
High `rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm` (>=3.559) further strengthens the colorectal/APC/high-WNT `dependency_KIF18A` subgroup but is not required for the dependency.

analyses.code: Three-predicate subgroup contrast: lineage == 'colorectal' && apc_mutation == 1 && wnt_activity_score >= 0.819.
analyses.result_summary: The colorectal/APC-mutant/high-WNT subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.1119 (n=3133) versus -0.2530 in all other lines; effect -0.8589.

analyses.code: Component subgroup contrasts for colorectal/APC/WNT combinations.
analyses.result_summary: Colorectal/APC-mutant but WNT-low lines had mean -0.2727 (effect +0.0347 versus rest, p=0.00573), and colorectal/APC-wildtype/high-WNT lines had mean -0.2718 (effect +0.0356, p=0.00384), showing the strong KIF18A dependency required both APC mutation and high WNT within colorectal lineage.

analyses.code: Four-predicate split of the colorectal/APC/high-WNT subgroup by rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm >= 3.559.
analyses.result_summary: Adding high ASCL2 gave mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.1414 (n=1836, effect -0.8664), while the same colorectal/APC/high-WNT subgroup with lower ASCL2 still had mean -1.0701 (n=1297, effect -0.7836).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `nf1_loss` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines without `nf1_loss`.
The `dependency_RIT1` effect is strongest in the joint subgroup `nf1_loss=1` and `lineage=brain`.
Cell lines with `cas9_activity_score` in the top quartile (>=1.080) have more negative essential-gene dependency scores, especially `dependency_POLR2A` and `dependency_TMED10`, than lower-Cas9 lines.

analyses.code: Two-sample mean contrast of dependency_RIT1 by nf1_loss.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_RIT1` was -0.3527 in `nf1_loss=1` lines (n=4560) versus -0.2572 otherwise; effect -0.0955.

analyses.code: Two-predicate subgroup contrast: nf1_loss == 1 && lineage == 'brain'.
analyses.result_summary: The `nf1_loss=1`/brain subgroup had mean `dependency_RIT1` -0.4047 (n=329) versus -0.2650 in all other lines; effect -0.1397.

analyses.code: Top-quartile subgroup contrasts for cas9_activity_score.
analyses.result_summary: High-Cas9 lines had more negative `dependency_POLR2A` (effect -0.0209, p=1.03e-8) and `dependency_TMED10` (effect -0.0205, p=1.31e-8), supporting a screen-quality covariate effect smaller than the growth-rate effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A systematic single-feature heterogeneity screen across all dependency outcomes will recover the strongest single subgroup predictor for each dependency outcome.
For `dependency_RPL11`, the strongest single-feature subgroup is `myc_amplification=1` or high `copy_number_8q24`, producing more negative dependency scores.
For `dependency_DHX9`, the strongest single-feature subgroup is high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` or high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm`, producing more negative dependency scores.

analyses.code: Scanned binary, categorical-level, and continuous quartile predicates against every dependency outcome.
analyses.result_summary: The top single-feature negative effects were BRAF by `braf_v600e=1` (-0.5552), EGFR by `egfr_amplification=1` (-0.3486), ERBB2 by `erbb2_amplification=1` (-0.4321), KRAS by `kras_mutation=1` (-0.3075), RIT1 by `nf1_loss=1` (-0.0955), KIF18A by `lineage=colorectal` (-0.4680), TMED10 by high growth (-0.0369), DHX9 by high growth (-0.0371), POLR2A by high growth (-0.1043), and RPL11 by `myc_amplification=1` (-0.1091).

analyses.code: Two-sample mean contrasts of dependency_RPL11 by myc_amplification and high copy_number_8q24.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_RPL11` was more negative in `myc_amplification=1` lines (effect -0.1091) and in high-`copy_number_8q24` lines (effect -0.0988), both p<1e-300.

analyses.code: Top-quartile contrasts for dependency_DHX9 using growth_rate_doublings_per_day and rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_DHX9` was more negative in high-growth lines (effect -0.0371, p<1e-300) and high-`rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` lines (effect -0.0286, p=2.0e-15).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A systematic two-feature dependency-heterogeneity screen will refine final dependency subgroups without changing the primary driver conclusions for canonical oncogene dependencies.
For `dependency_POLR2A`, the main heterogeneity pattern is high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, with two-feature subsets adding little beyond proliferation.
For `dependency_RPL11`, joint `myc_amplification=1` and high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` marks a more negative subgroup than either unmodeled screen average.

analyses.code: Exhaustive two-predicate subgroup scan over usable binary, categorical-level, and quartile predicates for each dependency outcome.
analyses.result_summary: Two-predicate scans preserved the canonical drivers: BRAF top subgroups all contained `braf_v600e=1`, EGFR top subgroups all contained `egfr_amplification=1`, ERBB2 top subgroups all contained `erbb2_amplification=1`, and KRAS top subgroups all contained `kras_mutation=1`.

analyses.code: Two-predicate POLR2A subgroup scan and comparison with single high-growth contrast.
analyses.result_summary: The best broad `dependency_POLR2A` signal remained high growth: high growth alone had effect -0.1043, while high growth plus lung/adenocarcinoma or high Cas9 yielded similar effects around -0.098 to -0.103; a small HER2-enriched/high-growth subset had effect -0.1174 but only n=252.

analyses.code: Two-predicate RPL11 subgroup contrast: myc_amplification == 1 && growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964.
analyses.result_summary: `myc_amplification=1` plus high growth had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3936 (n=2557) versus -0.2632 in all other lines; effect -0.1303.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_BRAF` is `braf_v600e=1`, with more negative `dependency_BRAF` than `braf_v600e=0`.
The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_EGFR` is `egfr_amplification=1`, with more negative `dependency_EGFR` than `egfr_amplification=0`.
The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_ERBB2` is `erbb2_amplification=1`, with more negative `dependency_ERBB2` than `erbb2_amplification=0`.
The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_KRAS` is `kras_mutation=1`, with more negative `dependency_KRAS` than `kras_mutation=0`.
The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_RIT1` is `nf1_loss=1`, especially `nf1_loss=1` and `lineage=brain`, with more negative `dependency_RIT1` than other lines.
The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_KIF18A` is `lineage=colorectal`, `apc_mutation=1`, and `wnt_activity_score>=0.819`, with more negative `dependency_KIF18A` than all other lines.
The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_TMED10` is high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (>=0.964), with more negative `dependency_TMED10` than slower-growing lines.

The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_DHX9` is high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` combined with high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` (>=5.669), with more negative `dependency_DHX9` than all other lines.
The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_POLR2A` is high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (>=0.964), with more negative `dependency_POLR2A` than slower-growing lines.

The final best-supported subgroup for `dependency_RPL11` is `myc_amplification=1` combined with high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (>=0.964), with more negative `dependency_RPL11` than all other lines.

analyses.code: Final selected subgroup contrast for dependency_BRAF.
analyses.result_summary: Final BRAF subgroup `braf_v600e=1`: effect -0.5552, means -0.8047 versus -0.2495.

analyses.code: Final selected subgroup contrast for dependency_EGFR.
analyses.result_summary: Final EGFR subgroup `egfr_amplification=1`: effect -0.3486, means -0.6010 versus -0.2524.

analyses.code: Final selected subgroup contrast for dependency_ERBB2.
analyses.result_summary: Final ERBB2 subgroup `erbb2_amplification=1`: effect -0.4321, means -0.6856 versus -0.2535.

analyses.code: Final selected subgroup contrast for dependency_KRAS.
analyses.result_summary: Final KRAS subgroup `kras_mutation=1`: effect -0.3075, means -0.5594 versus -0.2520.

analyses.code: Final selected subgroup contrasts for dependency_RIT1.
analyses.result_summary: Final RIT1 broad subgroup `nf1_loss=1`: effect -0.0955; refined `nf1_loss=1`/brain subgroup effect -0.1397.

analyses.code: Final selected three-predicate subgroup contrast for dependency_KIF18A.
analyses.result_summary: Final KIF18A subgroup `lineage=colorectal`, `apc_mutation=1`, `wnt_activity_score>=0.819`: effect -0.8589, means -1.1119 versus -0.2530.

analyses.code: Final selected subgroup contrast for dependency_TMED10.
analyses.result_summary: Final TMED10 subgroup high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`: effect -0.0369, means -0.2816 versus -0.2447.

analyses.code: Final selected two-predicate subgroup contrast for dependency_DHX9.
analyses.result_summary: Final DHX9 subgroup high growth plus high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm`: effect -0.0595, means -0.3892 versus -0.3297.

analyses.code: Final selected subgroup contrast for dependency_POLR2A.
analyses.result_summary: Final POLR2A subgroup high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`: effect -0.1043, means -0.4972 versus -0.3928.

analyses.code: Final selected two-predicate subgroup contrast for dependency_RPL11.
analyses.result_summary: Final RPL11 subgroup `myc_amplification=1` plus high growth: effect -0.1303, means -0.3936 versus -0.2632.

final_dependency_subgroups.dependency_RIT1: `nf1_loss=1`, strongest in `nf1_loss=1` and `lineage=brain` (broad effect -0.0955; refined effect -0.1397)
